title: Carol Kirkwood Says Goodbye to Her Weather Job
summary: Carol Kirkwood has worked for the BBC for 28 years, waking up very early at 2:45am to tell people the weather on TV. She is now retiring, which means she is stopping her job. She says she will miss her alarm clock and her friends at work. Carol loved meeting nice people while reporting the weather from places like Wimbledon and the Chelsea Flower Show. She got special gifts like a gold poppy and a weather station named after her, which made her feel happy and appreciated. She is 63 years old and from Scotland. Carol is sad to leave but excited for what comes next.

background_read: Carol Kirkwood is a famous weather presenter on BBC Breakfast, a morning TV show in the UK. She has been telling people the weather for many years, starting her job in 2010 and working at the BBC for 28 years total. Weather presenters like Carol help viewers plan their day by sharing if it will be sunny, rainy, or snowy. She is known for her friendly style and has reported from big events like Wimbledon, where tennis is played, and the Chelsea Flower Show, which is about flowers. Retiring means she is leaving her job to enjoy other things in life, and she feels emotional about saying goodbye to her colleagues and viewers who watched her every morning.
Article_Structure: WHO: Carol Kirkwood, a weather presenter for the BBC
WHAT: She is retiring after 28 years of waking up early to report the weather
WHERE: She worked for BBC Breakfast in the UK and reported from places like Wimbledon and the Tower of London
WHY: She wants to stop her early mornings and will miss her colleagues, but feels appreciated by the kindness shown to her

perspectives.description: She is happy to retire but sad to leave her job and friends, and grateful for the appreciation from people.
